title: UK Allows US Use of Bases to Destroy Iranian Missile Depots
description: The UK has allowed the US to make use of British military bases for the specific purpose of destroying Iranian missile depots, Prime Minister Keir Starmer said.

title: Sources of Variation in India’s Foreign Exchange Reserves during April-December 2025
description: Today, the Reserve Bank of India released the balance of payments (BoP) data for the third quarter (Q3), i.e., October-December of 2025-26, on its website (www.rbi.org.in). On the basis of these data, the sources of variation in foreign exchange reserves during April-December 2025 are detailed below in Table 1. title: Developments in India’s Balance of Payments during the Third Quarter (October-December) of 2025-26
description: Preliminary data on India’s balance of payments (BoP) for the third quarter (Q3), i.e., October-December 2025-26, are presented in Statements I and II. Key Features of India’s BoP in Q3:2025-26 India’s current account deficit increased to US$ 13.2 billion (1.3 per cent of GDP) in Q3:2025-26 from US$ 11.3 billion (1.1 per cent of GDP) in Q3:2024-25 (Table 1).1,2 Merchandise trade deficit at US$ 93.6 billion in Q3:2025-26 was higher than US$ 79.3 billion in Q3:2024-25. Net servic

title: Shashi Tharoor questions ‘triple-engine’ governance model, flags concerns over federal fairness post 2014
description: Shashi Tharoor questions the efficiency and fairness of India's 'triple-engine' governance model, arguing that political alignment may hinder project execution and undermine competitive federalism.

title: Withdrawal of ₹2000 Denomination Banknotes – Status
description: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) had announced the withdrawal of ₹2000 denomination banknotes from circulation vide Press Release 2023-2024/257 dated May 19, 2023. The status of withdrawal of ₹2000 banknotes is periodically published by the RBI. The last press release in this regard was published on February 02, 2026. 2. title: Iran’s Missile Barrage Tests Whether US Has Enough Interceptors
description: The ability of the US, Israel and Gulf Arab states to weather Iran’s retaliatory strikes will depend on how many missile interceptors they have — and stocks are most likely dangerously low after intense combat with the Islamic Republic last year.

title: Trump Says He Sees Possible ‘Friendly Takeover of Cuba’
description: US President Donald Trump suggested a possible “friendly takeover of Cuba” as the White House looks to leverage the Communist-run island’s energy crisis to exert greater influence.

title: Tollywood actor Koel Mallick, Babul Supriyo, ex-Bengal DGP Rajeev Kumar among TMC nominees for RS polls
description: Supriyo, who joined the TMC in 2021, is currently a minister in the Mamata Banerjee cabinet. Kumar served as the Director General of Police and earlier as the Commissioner of Kolkata Police. Guruswamy is a Supreme Court advocate, while Mallick is a prominent actor in Tollywood

title: Watch: AAP chief Kejriwal breaks down, sobs after discharge in Delhi excise policy case
description: AAP chief Arvind Kejriwal and former Delhi Deputy Chief Minister Manish Sisodia were discharged in the Delhi excise policy case by the Rouse Avenue Court on Friday, with the court rejecting the CBI's allegations. Here's what the two AAP leaders had to say.

title: Indiana Bears? Illinois Fights Back With Bill to Hold Onto Team
description: The battle to lure the Chicago Bears is heating up as lawmakers in their home state of Illinois voted in favor of a measure that could pave the way for the team to build a new suburban stadium.

title: 'Unabashed defence of his host': Congress slams Modi's Knesset address, invokes Einstein's letter to Nehru in 1947
description: The Congress accused PM Modi of defending Netanyahu's policies and recalled Nehru's historical stance on the Israeli-Palestinian conflict. It highlighted Nehru's empathy for both Jews and Arabs, stressing the need for mutual respect and understanding to achieve peace.

title: US Levels New Sanctions on Iran’s Missile Program, Shadow Fleet
description: The Trump administration imposed sanctions on more than 30 entities that support Iranian oil and weapons sales, ramping up pressure on Tehran amid nuclear talks and the looming threat of US airstrikes.

title: Priyanka Gandhi Vadra urges PM Modi to speak on Gaza ‘genocide’ in Knesset address during Israel visit
description: As Prime Minister Modi embarks on his second visit to Israel, Congress leader Priyanka Gandhi urges him to address the Gaza conflict and highlight the plight of innocent victims during his Knesset speech, asserting India's commitment to truth and justice on the global stage.

title: EU Chief Says €90 Billion for Kyiv to Come ‘One Way or Another’
description: European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen said the bloc will deliver on its €90 billion ($106 billion) loan package to Ukraine “one way or another” as the war-battered nation is set to run out of funds in a matter of weeks.

title: Who is Uday Bhanu Chib? Youth Congress President arrested over shirtless protest during AI Impact Summit
description: Uday Bhanu Chib, the National President of the Indian Youth Congress, was arrested for a shirtless protest during the AI Impact Summit in Delhi. The protest has sparked debate on the boundaries of political expression and the implications for youth activism in India.

title: UK Police Arrest Peter Mandelson on Suspicion of Misconduct
description: UK police arrested Labour Party grandee Peter Mandelson on suspicion of misconduct in public office after the US Department of Justice released emails allegedly showing him forwarding government information to convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein.

title: 'Alliance will be gone without Mamata': Mani Shankar Aiyar’s scathing warning to INDIA bloc, Rahul Gandhi
description: Senior Congress leader Mani Shankar Aiyar advocates for Mamata Banerjee to lead the opposition INDIA bloc, emphasising her crucial role amidst political tensions. His remarks spark debate about leadership dynamics as elections loom, raising questions about the future of the alliance against the BJP.

title: Iran-US Nuclear Talks to Resume This Week in Geneva
description: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi said he’s likely to meet US special envoy Steve Witkoff Thursday in Geneva, seeking a diplomatic resolution for the latest standoff over Tehran’s nuclear program.

title: PM Modi slams ‘shirtless’ protest at AI Impact Summit; tells Congress, ‘country knows you are already naked…’
description: The entire country was filled with pride. But what did the Congress and its ecosystem do? The Congress turned a global event for India into a platform for its dirty and naked politics. Congress leaders reached the venue naked in front of the foreign guests.&nbsp;

title: World leaders scope out US’ next steps after Trump tariff Loss
description: Governments around the world reacted with caution after the US Supreme Court invalidated President Donald Trump’s broad emergency tariffs, with some reaffirming existing agreements and others taking a wait-and-see approach to evaluate his next steps.

title: CRIME REVIEW FOR THE YEAR 2026
description: Police Department - Police Computer Wing: CRIME REVIEW FOR THE YEAR 2026

title: Final electoral roll published in Kerala; around 9 lakhs names removed
description: The ECI released Kerala's final electoral roll, removing about nine lakh names. The updated voter count is over 2.69 crore. Details here.

title: Trump administration eases limits on coal plants for emitting mercury, other toxins
description: Trump administration eases limits on coal plants for emitting mercury, other toxins

title: Who is Vinod Jakhar? 5 things to know about the new NSUI national president
description: Vinod Jakhar has been appointed as the new National President of the NSUI, marking a historic first for Rajasthan. With a strong commitment to student rights and social justice, he aims to uphold democratic values and lead millions of students across India.

title: AI Summit disrupted as Indian Youth Congress workers stage topless protest, chant anti-Modi slogans | Watch Video
description: Indian Youth Congress staged a topless protest at Bharat Mandapam during the AI Impact Summit 2026, chanting slogans against Narendra Modi. Delhi Police detained several workers, while BJP and Congress leaders traded sharp remarks.

title: IEA Meeting Ends Without Unity as US Pushes to Scrap Net Zero
description: The conflicting positions of US President Donald Trump and major European governments came to the fore at a meeting of the west’s most important energy forecaster.

title: Bhupen Borah reveals what Rahul Gandhi said on call after he resigned: ‘Said he felt humiliated too’
description: Bhupen Borah said that when Rahul Gandhi called, he raised the issue of how he had felt humiliated within the party. At this, Rahul Gandhi told Bhupen Bohra that he, too, has felt humiliated.

title: Trump ‘grasping for excuses' to slap ‘outrageous’ tariffs on India — US lawmaker says ‘ally’ singled out
description: Congressman Brad Sherman's comments come weeks after India and the US announced in a joint statement that they had reached an interim framework for a long-awaited trade deal, which lowered reciprocal tariffs on Indian goods and services from 25% to 18%.

title: Pritzker Proposes $56 Billion Budget Amid Threat of Federal Cuts
description: Illinois Governor JB Pritzker proposed an approximately $56 billion budget for the coming year as the state faces higher costs and more uncertainty around federal funding under President Donald Trump.

title: Mamdani Plans to Hike NYC Property Tax to Fill Budget Hole
description: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani is proposing raising New York City’s property taxes for the first time in more than two decades and is looking to raid the city’s reserve funds to fill a roughly $5 billion budget gap.

title: 5 explosive remarks by Mani Shankar Aiyar that rocked Congress – Aurangzeb's ‘ascension’ to Chinese ‘invasion’
description: Congress veteran Mani Shankar Aiyar is at it again. In a latest outburst, he has criticised prominent party members while predicting a victory for the LDF in Kerala. Aiyar's past controversies add to the intrigue.

title: Trump Says Government Won’t Pay for NJ-NY Tunnel ‘Cost Overruns’
description: The federal government won’t cover any cost overruns on a $16 billion Hudson River rail tunnel project for which the White House previously froze funding, President Donald Trump warned on Monday.

title: Congress says Bhupen Borah withdrew resignation, he says 'sought time to reconsider' - What we know so far
description: Borah told reporters in Guwahati that he sent his resignation to the Congress high command at 8 a.m. in the morning, mentioning in detail why he took the step

title: ‘Kick his backside…’: Mani Shankar Aiyar fires retort over Pawan Khera's ‘no connection with Congress’ remark
description: Aiyar served as the Union minister for panchayati raj during the Congress-led UPA government. He was suspended from December 2017 after a controversial remark about PM Modi. The suspension was revoked August 2018

title: Priyank Kharge accuses RSS of ‘money laundering’, BJP responds – ‘spitting at the sky’
description: Karnataka Minister Priyank Kharge accused the RSS of money laundering, prompting sharp responses from BJP leaders. The debate spirals into a discussion on regional development and political accountability.

title: Mint Explainer | Bangladesh’s political transition and what it means for India
description: India could focus on improving bilateral economic ties with Bangladesh that have been a major casualty under Yunus's governance. Embargoes on the usage of Indian land ports could be lifted as a goodwill gesture.

title: Former Assam Congress chief Bhupen Bora resigns from party ahead of assembly polls: Report

title: Netanyahu Says Iran Deal Has to Strip Away Nuclear Capabilities

title: How can India Create More Jobs? Unleashing the 1.4 billion: A Blueprint for India’s Job-Led Growth
description: India stands at a historic threshold. As the world’s youngest nation with a median age of just under 29 years, it possesses a demographic engine that could power global growth for decades. Yet, as the country approaches the centenary of our independence, it confronts the “2047 Paradox”: a “Great Decoupling” where soaring GDP growth is […] The post How can India Create More Jobs? Unleashing the 1.4 billion: A Blueprint for India’s Job-Led Growth appeared first on CPR

title: Trade, an Increasingly Important Pillar in Dhampur’s Economy
description: In the ancient times, trade was the primary economic activity which led to the establishment of urban settlements, where agricultural surplus was exchanged for manufactured or imported goods like iron, stone, wood, agricultural implements, textiles, spices, etc. Over time these small market-towns also became industrial centers where artisans and crafts persons produced a range of […] The post Trade, an Increasingly Important Pillar in Dhampur’s Economy appeared first on CPR.

title: TREADS Dialogues I — The Institutional Question: Water Security for Viksit Bharat
description: India’s water crisis persists not due to lack of policies or resources, but due to institutional fragmentation, short-termism, and the absence of coordination. Without institutional convergence at the national level, water security goals for Viksit Bharat 2047 will continue to be undermined. Water risk governance lies at the core of India’s water security challenge The […] The post TREADS Dialogues I — The Institutional Question: Water Security for Viksit Bharat appeared first on CPR.

title: From Touring Cinema to Home Theaters: The Changing Face of Mass Entertainment in Dhampur
description: Imagine the sun setting over an open field, a film projector being set up, a screen being tied between poles by two young men, and as the night sky darkens a large number of men and children taking their seats on the ground for an evening of entertainment. Tickets were cheap at around four to […] The post From Touring Cinema to Home Theaters: The Changing Face of Mass Entertainment in Dhampur appeared first on CPR.

description: Founded on 11/11/2014 at Harvard Law School, and incubated at the Centre for Policy Research, LRI turns 11 today! title: Flooding Disrupts Life in Dhampur and Surrounding Villages Once More: Why do we Fail in Flood Mitigation?
description: The monsoon rains bring a respite from the scorching summer heat and promise of a good harvest, but it also brings perils of inundation that disrupt life and livelihoods. This monsoon season, 47 people have reportedly died in Bijnor district due to flooding.
